description: PhD-level marketing knowledge taxonomy — 18 domains, 234 topics, 5 content types, 4 verticals = 4,680 unique generation slots

verticals.label: IBEW 134 Electrical Contractors
verticals.context: union electrical contractors, NEC code compliance, prevailing wage projects, commercial/residential electrical

verticals.label: General Contractors / HVAC / Plumbing
verticals.context: licensed general contractors, HVAC technicians, plumbers, multi-trade firms

verticals.label: SMB SaaS & Professional Services
verticals.context: small business software companies, consultants, agencies, professional service firms

verticals.label: Platform & Marketplace Operators
verticals.context: two-sided marketplaces, contractor platforms, service booking apps

contentTypes.label: Strategic Advisory
contentTypes.prompt: A {vertical} business owner asks for strategic guidance on {topic}. Provide a framework-driven response with KPIs, implementation steps, compliance guardrails, and risk assessment.

contentTypes.label: Campaign Blueprint
contentTypes.prompt: Design a complete campaign for a {vertical} business applying {topic} principles. Include channel selection, budget allocation, timeline, A/B test hypotheses, stop-loss criteria, and expected ROI benchmarks.

contentTypes.label: Diagnostic Analysis
contentTypes.prompt: A {vertical} business is underperforming in the area of {topic}. Provide root cause analysis with evidence-based checklist, industry benchmarks, remediation plan with rollback triggers, and compliance review.

contentTypes.label: Content Artifact
contentTypes.prompt: Create a marketing content piece (blog post, email sequence, ad copy, or case study) for a {vertical} business applying {topic}. Include measurable CTAs, conversion KPIs, and compliance disclaimers.

contentTypes.label: Anti-Pattern Alert
contentTypes.prompt: A {vertical} business is about to make a common mistake related to {topic}. Identify failure signatures, early warning metrics, historical evidence of what goes wrong, risk assessment, and better alternatives with guardrails.

domains.name: Marketing Strategy Foundations

Value chain analysis for service businesses

Product-market fit assessment frameworks
Blue ocean strategy applied to underserved trade verticals
Strategic group mapping for local service providers

Ansoff matrix decision framework for SMB growth
Resource-based view of sustainable competitive advantage

Social proof deployment for contractor credibility and trust

Choice architecture for service tier selection

Endowment effect in subscription and retainer pricing

Default bias in package structuring and upsells
Peak-end rule in customer experience design
